codeReview.summary: The semantic-path matcher and app-level characterization move attribution beyond pathname-only matching, but the implementation over-normalizes repeated/CSV query values by deduplicating them. That makes requests which are not semantically equivalent to the declared prewarm target still count as matches, so warm-start attribution can remain incorrect for duplicated selector inputs.

issues.description: `normalizePrewarmQueryValues()` deduplicates query values with `new Set(...)` before comparison, so a request like `/simple/price?ids=bitcoin,bitcoin&vs_currencies=usd` normalizes to the same target signature as the declared prewarm target `/simple/price?ids=bitcoin&vs_currencies=usd`. The feature requires attribution only for requests truly equivalent to the prewarm target semantics, but duplicated selector values are not proven equivalent here and can still claim warm-path evidence.

The new characterization covers reordered matching parameters and a broader mismatched selector set, but it does not cover duplicate-value or repeated-key requests, so the over-normalization bug in `matchStartupPrewarmTarget()` is left uncharacterized.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, skill, and commit `84c94b0`. The pathname-only attribution bug was partially fixed, but the new matcher still collapses duplicate query values and can misattribute warm-start benefit to non-equivalent requests, so the review fails on a blocking semantic-matching bug.
